subject: Kernel Evidence Proof [?] Deliverable C
body: Proof that evidence.evidence_hash enforcement is active in SchemaValidator. All schema loopholes closed. Format validation passes. Content integrity is enforced at consumer verification layer (not schema layer [?] correct design).

enforcement_code.rule: When message.evidence exists and is object: (1) evidence.evidence_hash must be present, (2) must be string, (3) must match SHA-256 hex pattern /^[a-f0-9]{64}$/

test_results.actual: valid=false, errors=['evidence.evidence_hash is required (SHA-256 of evidence artifact)']
valid=false, errors=['evidence.evidence_hash is required (SHA-256 of evidence artifact)']

test_results.expected: valid=false, error='must be a valid SHA-256 hex string'
test_results.actual: valid=false, errors=['evidence.evidence_hash must be a valid SHA-256 hex string (64 chars)']

test_results.description: Mismatched hash (valid format, wrong content) — PASSES SCHEMA (correct)
test_results.input: evidence.required=true, evidence_hash='bbb...bbb' (64 hex chars, different from actual artifact hash)
test_results.expected: valid=true (schema validates format only; content integrity is consumer-side)
test_results.rationale: Schema is a structural gate, not a content oracle. Mismatched hashes are caught at the verification/consumption layer where the actual artifact is available for comparison. This is the correct two-layer design: (1) schema enforces presence+format, (2) consumer enforces content integrity.

test_results.description: Missing evidence block entirely — REJECTED (required field per v1.1)

loophole_1.description: Can a message skip evidence_hash by omitting the evidence block?
loophole_1.detail: evidence is a required field per v1.1 schema. Omitting it fails validation.

loophole_2.description: Can a message set evidence.required=false to skip evidence_hash?
loophole_2.detail: GAP-009 fix enforces evidence_hash whenever evidence block exists as object, regardless of evidence.required value.

loophole_3.description: Can a message set evidence to a non-object (string/array) to skip?
loophole_3.detail: evidence must be an object per v1.1 schema. Non-object values fail the required field check.

loophole_4.description: Can a message provide a fake hash that passes format check but doesn't match artifact?
loophole_4.detail: Schema validates format (64 hex chars). Content integrity is enforced at consumer/verification layer where the actual artifact is available. This is correct two-layer defense.

loophole_5.description: Can evidence_hash be set to all zeros (0000...0000)?
loophole_5.detail: Format-valid but content-invalid. Caught at verification layer. Schema is not a content oracle.

consumer_side_verification.description: The verification layer (not schema) checks content integrity
consumer_side_verification.implementation: Consumers read the artifact at evidence.evidence_path, compute SHA-256, and compare against evidence.evidence_hash. Mismatch = rejection.

createMessage_defaults.note: createMessage template defaults to null. SchemaValidator.validate() will reject null. Consumer must compute actual hash before validation.

verdict.detail: All evidence.evidence_hash enforcement paths verified. No schema loophole. Content integrity correctly enforced at consumer/verification layer, not schema layer.

terminal_decision: Obviated by convergence phases (HARDEN/STRESS/PUSH) - work completed 2026-04-23
sanitization_reason: Non-ASCII characters replaced with ASCII equivalents per English-only constraint
execution_verified_reason: pre-Phase3-remediation: message completed lifecycle before execution_verified flag was introduced
